verdict.note: Verified by inspection + clean vite build: close button no longer hardcodes setSel('new'); a useEffect mirrors active's validity test (sel!=='graph' && !order.includes(sel) -> 'new'), so a closed/vanished session only bumps you to New Session if sel still points at it, else your manual switch stands. Live destructive browser close NOT run: the only sessions on :8787 are real in-flight work (closing removes their worktrees). Full browser YATU deferred pending disposable sessions.

Re-measure at HEAD 085a528 after removing the left list's '// <project> sessions' header (si-list-head div + dead .si-list-head CSS, the dead 'project' prop thread Dashboard->SessionInterface, and the orphaned session.title i18n key). Orthogonal to the close-tab-fallback contract: no tab-close button, selection state, or the event-driven useEffect fallback was touched. Prior inspection verdict still holds — close no longer hardcodes setSel('new'); the useEffect bumps to New Session only when sel still points at a vanished/closed session, else your manual switch stands. Clean vite build passes. Live destructive browser run still deferred: the only sessions on :8787 are real in-flight work whose worktrees a close would remove.

Live browser YATU (worktree vite :5190 -> backend :8787, real board). BEFORE/AFTER: on main the header action row = [⌨ nav, close(.si-act.kill)]; on this change = [⌨ nav] only (hasKill true->false). The misleading 'close' header button is GONE. Surviving close path verified live: right-click a session row -> menu shows rename+close -> clicking close raises the 'removes its worktree' confirm; cancelled with Esc (non-destructive). Destructive Pass1/Pass2 fallback landing NOT re-run — the only :8787 sessions are real in-flight work whose worktrees a real close would remove — and the event-driven fallback (useEffect bumps to New only when sel still points at a vanished session, else your switch stands) is untouched by this change; it only removed the header entry point.

Re-measure at HEAD 0d92754 after the /exit interception. Orthogonal to the close-tab-fallback contract: the new sendMsg guard only adds a NEW trigger that calls the existing act('close'); it touches neither the header Close button, act(), the close endpoint, nor the event-driven useEffect tab-fallback. Prior inspection verdict still holds — close no longer hardcodes setSel('new'); the useEffect bumps to New Session only when sel still points at a vanished session, else your manual switch stands. Clean vite build passes. Live destructive 2-session browser run still deferred: the only sessions on :8787 are real in-flight work whose worktrees a real close would remove (the /exit measurement above intercepted /close to stay non-destructive).

Orthogonal to the close-tab-fallback contract. The status-word-colour change touches only the status LABEL colour (the STATUS_COLOR map in session.js plus an inline color on the status span); it does not touch the header action row, the close action, the close endpoint, or the event-driven tab-fallback useEffect. Contract holds in spirit: closing stays row-menu-only behind a confirm, and the fallback bumps to New Session only when the selection still points at a vanished session. Clean vite build passes and the SessionWindow renders (status-word-colour filed pass with live before/after evidence). Live destructive two-session run still deferred: the only :8787 sessions are real in-flight work whose worktrees a real close would remove.

Orthogonal to the exit-closes contract. The status-word-colour change only colours the status LABEL; it does not touch the input box, the exit interception, the send-message path, or the close action. Prior pass holds in spirit. Re-run deferred because typing exit closes a live work session. Clean vite build passes.

Clean vite build passes; SessionWindow renders. Live destructive two-session run still deferred (the only :8787 sessions are real in-flight work).

Orthogonal to the SessionRow face change. It does not touch the ❯ input box, the client-side /exit interception, the control-socket dispatch, or the close action — all in SessionInterface.jsx, untouched on this branch. Prior pass holds.
